title: Gone Maggie Gone

jokes.text: A solar eclipse is like a woman breast-feeding in a restaurant. It's free, it's beautiful, but under no circumstances should you look at it.
jokes.setup: Need to explain why you shouldn't look at a solar eclipse
jokes.punchline: Unexpected comparison to breastfeeding in public with perfect parallel structure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy with perfect three-part structure. Original, unexpected comparison that works on multiple levels. Classic Simpsons wit.

jokes.text: This was supposed to be pre-made. Fine, I'll do it myself. Where is the end of the friggin' tape? Forget it! We'll do it next week! The eclipse is today. There's an eclipse when I say there's an eclipse!
jokes.setup: News anchor trying to demonstrate eclipse viewer on live TV
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown culminating in delusion of controlling celestial events
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from minor technical difficulty to megalomaniacal breakdown. Character-specific comedy gold.

jokes.text: Hit the road, you big load!
jokes.setup: Kids fighting over eclipse viewer
jokes.punchline: Bart's creative rhyming insult
jokes.explanation: Solid Bart-style insult with nice rhyme scheme. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: There'll be another one in North Yemen in 2027.
jokes.setup: Lisa worries Marge will miss the eclipse
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact suggestion of traveling to war-torn Yemen for next eclipse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge-style deadpan delivery with absurd specificity. Shows her practical yet naive worldview.

jokes.text: I finally feel like part of a family.
jokes.setup: Everyone enjoying the eclipse together
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's pathetic admission of loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Comic Book Guy's social isolation. Character-specific pathos that's funny and sad.

jokes.text: Just one little peek. It's beautiful.
jokes.setup: After being warned not to look at the eclipse
jokes.punchline: Marge does exactly what she was told not to do, with immediate consequences
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with Marge's characteristic rule-breaking impulse. Predictable but well-executed.

jokes.text: Don't worry, honey. I'll be your seeing-eye Homer. We're in some kind of doctor room, bunch of stuff on the walls. It's kinda hard to describe. I'm gonna take my break now.
jokes.setup: Homer offering to help blind Marge navigate
jokes.punchline: Immediate abandonment after useless description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness and incompetence. The escalation from helpful to abandonment is beautifully character-driven.

jokes.text: Any stress could increase the blood pressure in Marge's eyeballs, leading to what we call Tex Avery Syndrome.
jokes.setup: Medical explanation of Marge's eye condition
jokes.punchline: Reference to cartoon physics where eyes bulge out dramatically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-reference to animation history. Tex Avery was famous for exaggerated cartoon eye bulging. Smart cultural reference.

jokes.text: Wolves are taking all our women!
jokes.setup: Homer sees cartoon-style wolf reaction to seeing women
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets animated wolf behavior as literal threat
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of cartoon wolf whistle behavior is perfectly in character and absurd.

jokes.text: You guys have treated me like a princess! Everyday's been like the first ten minutes of Mother's Day.
jokes.setup: Marge expressing gratitude for family's care
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to how Mother's Day attention quickly fades
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about family dynamics. The specificity of 'first ten minutes' makes it land perfectly.

jokes.text: Is there any other way to enter a kitchen?
jokes.setup: Bart urgently tells Homer to come to the kitchen quick
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal-minded response about kitchen entry methods
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal-mindedness. Not groundbreaking but perfectly in character and well-timed.

jokes.text: Rats in the kitchen! Just like that delightful movie I taped in the theatre.
jokes.setup: Discovering rats in kitchen
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of movie piracy while referencing Ratatouille
jokes.explanation: Two jokes in one: Pixar reference and Homer's casual criminality. The juxtaposition of 'delightful' with piracy is perfect.

jokes.text: He's controlling me with my hair! Using me to cook a delicious sole meunière with a celery root purée, And carrots vichy.
jokes.setup: Rat controlling Bart like in Ratatouille
jokes.punchline: Bart somehow knows sophisticated French cuisine terminology
jokes.explanation: Absurd that Bart would know French culinary terms. The contrast between his character and sophisticated cooking knowledge is brilliant.

jokes.text: This is good. Good, but not great.
jokes.setup: Eating food prepared by rat-controlled Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual food criticism in bizarre circumstances
jokes.explanation: Homer's food obsession overriding concern for the supernatural situation. Perfect character priority inversion.

jokes.text: Here you go. This'll kill those dirty rats, and the clean ones, too.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for rat poison
jokes.punchline: Apu's inclusive approach to rat extermination
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay on 'dirty rats' idiom. Apu's thorough customer service approach applied to pest control.

jokes.text: What is it with kids and candy-colored poison, huh?
jokes.setup: Maggie reaching for poison that looks like candy
jokes.punchline: Apu's casual observation about dangerous product design
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about product design dangers. Apu's matter-of-fact delivery of a genuinely concerning observation.

jokes.text: Not in front of the poison man.
jokes.setup: Santa's Little Helper biting someone
jokes.punchline: Homer's concern about appearances in front of store clerk
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced social consciousness. Treating Apu as 'the poison man' shows his simple categorization of people.

jokes.text: Why did I bring the baby and the dog to the poison store?
jokes.setup: Chaos with baby and dog in poison store
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization of obviously poor planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - hindsight realization of obviously terrible decision. Classic character-driven comedy.

jokes.text: I can't leave the baby alone with the poison, and I can't leave the dog alone with the baby.
jokes.setup: Homer working through the logic puzzle
jokes.punchline: Applying classic puzzle logic to his specific dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Smart adaptation of fox-chicken-grain puzzle with Homer's specific items. Shows writers' cleverness in classical reference.

jokes.text: Can't someone help me with this puzzle?
jokes.setup: Homer struggling with logic puzzle
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall of puzzle-solving to ask for help
jokes.explanation: Homer's helplessness is in character but not particularly clever. Sets up Professor Frink appearance.

jokes.text: Professor Frink, help me figure this out!
jokes.setup: Homer calling for help with logic puzzle
jokes.punchline: Appealing to the town's science expert
jokes.explanation: Logical character choice but setup for bigger payoff. Not funny on its own but serves story function.

jokes.text: Great time to nap, Einstein!
jokes.setup: Professor Frink is asleep when Homer needs help
jokes.punchline: Homer's sarcastic Einstein reference to sleeping scientist
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm with Einstein reference. Functional but not inspired. Homer's frustration is believable.

jokes.text: Were you listening at all?
jokes.setup: Homer gives detailed solution to puzzle
jokes.punchline: Someone (probably dog or baby) clearly wasn't paying attention
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat. Homer expecting comprehension from baby/dog is mildly amusing but predictable.

jokes.text: Sir, I'd be obliged to you if you'd help me puzzle out my fox, duck and corn situation.
jokes.setup: Another character appears with same logic puzzle
jokes.punchline: Using classic puzzle terminology and old-timey speech
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to original puzzle format. The formal speech pattern adds character charm to the reference.

jokes.text: Well, the puzzle done puzzled itself out.
jokes.setup: Old man's fox, duck, and corn puzzle
jokes.punchline: Folksy way of saying the animals resolved it themselves (probably ate each other)
jokes.explanation: Charming wordplay with dark implication. The folksy speech pattern makes the grim resolution funnier.

jokes.text: And I'll be watching you too in case God's busy making tornadoes or not existing.
jokes.setup: Homer leaving Maggie 'under God's watchful eyes'
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of God's potential non-existence as backup plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor. Covers all theological bases while maintaining Homer's practical approach to divine delegation. Perfect economy and edge.

jokes.text: Catholics!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes the building is a Catholic convent
jokes.punchline: Single-word reaction of recognition/mild disdain
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment. Homer's religious prejudices revealed in single word. Economic but basic.

jokes.text: 'Cos she was fighting my dog, and stealing my poison.
jokes.setup: Nun asks why Homer left Maggie at convent
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact explanation of baby's criminal behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Treating baby like criminal while missing the real issue. Perfect Homer logic and delivery.

jokes.text: Now give her back so I can take her home to my rat-infested house!
jokes.setup: Homer demanding Maggie back from nuns
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently making case against his parenting with honest description
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-sabotage. Homer's honesty undermines his argument while he remains oblivious. Classic character comedy.

jokes.text: I'm married to Jesus! Yeah, right. And I'm married to Wonder Woman!
jokes.setup: Nun explains her marital status to Jesus
jokes.punchline: Homer's skeptical comparison to fictional marriage
jokes.explanation: Homer missing the metaphorical nature of religious vows. Wonder Woman reference shows his cultural priorities. Solid character humor.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Oh, no. Oh, no.
jokes.setup: Bart suggests someone go undercover as a nun
jokes.punchline: Lisa's growing horror at realization she'll have to do it
jokes.explanation: Standard escalating dread reaction. Lisa's intelligence makes her realize the implications faster. Functional setup.

jokes.text: There is no way Homer Simpson's gonna put on a dress, and act like a nun.
jokes.setup: Homer firmly rejecting cross-dressing plan
jokes.punchline: Sets up expectation that someone else will do it
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection setup. Homer's specific denial creates expectation he might actually do it. Standard but effective.

jokes.text: Told you there was no way I would do it. Are you sure you don't want me to do it? 'Cos I can do it. I got the outfit right here.
jokes.setup: Homer's firm refusal to dress as nun
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal with prepared costume revealing he always intended to do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff. Homer's enthusiasm despite protests, plus having costume ready, reveals his true eagerness. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm a poor little sinner who's lost her way. May I join your order?
jokes.setup: Homer disguised as nun trying to infiltrate convent
jokes.punchline: Overly theatrical religious language delivered by Homer in drag
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at religious speak is amusingly overwrought. The visual of him in nun outfit adds to absurdity.

jokes.text: Not like Sister Marilyn.
jokes.setup: Warning about modest habit wearing
jokes.punchline: Reference to nun who clearly dresses immodestly (likely Marilyn Monroe reference)
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag reference to Marilyn Monroe. Juxtaposition of sexy icon with nun creates humor through incongruity.

jokes.text: If you're happy and you know it That's a sin
jokes.setup: Traditional children's song in convent nursery
jokes.punchline: Twisted ending that happiness itself is sinful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of beloved children's song. Dark religious humor with perfect meter and timing. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Well next week's nine months after the prom. They'll be stacked up to the ceiling.
jokes.setup: Asked about more babies at orphanage
jokes.punchline: Cynical calculation of teen pregnancy timing after prom season
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about teen pregnancy cycles. Mother Superior's pragmatic cynicism is both funny and disturbing. Sharp social observation.

jokes.text: Well, more of a Curious George.
jokes.setup: Mother Superior calls Homer a 'Doubting Thomas'
jokes.punchline: Homer's reference to children's book character instead of biblical figure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing religious reference for pop culture one. Shows his intellectual priorities and cultural knowledge gaps.

jokes.text: Well, little monkey, it's time for you to spend some time with the man in the yellow hat.
jokes.setup: Homer's Curious George reference
jokes.punchline: Mother Superior continuing the children's book metaphor as threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns Homer's innocent reference into ominous threat. Mother Superior's quick wit makes it work.

jokes.text: Because I'm sick of that Mother Superior. Whenever we play football, she has to be quarterback.
jokes.setup: Sister helping Lisa with mysterious rebellion
jokes.punchline: Petty sports complaint about authoritarian religious leader
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Reducing complex theological rebellion to schoolyard sports grievance. Perfect character motivation trivialization.

jokes.text: And before you decide to become a nun, consider this: I am only 23!
jokes.setup: Young sister giving Lisa advice about nun life
jokes.punchline: Shocking revelation of premature aging from religious life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about religious life's toll. Shocking age revelation creates effective punchline. Good character moment and social commentary.

jokes.text: That's Latin! Deum is God. And of course quaerite means seek.
jokes.setup: Lisa encountering Latin clue
jokes.punchline: Lisa's intellectual excitement and knowledge showing off
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - intellectual enthusiasm and showing off knowledge. True to character but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: Mommy's little baby is getting big and chubby. Who's a chubby baby?
jokes.setup: Marge baby-talking to what she thinks is Maggie
jokes.punchline: Talking to substitute baby (likely Bart) in typical baby voice
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity humor. Marge's maternal instincts versus reality creates mild comedy. Setup for bigger reveals.

jokes.text: Cool it, she's getting suspicious. You cool it! I'm hungry!
jokes.setup: Bart and dog pretending to be baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Argument between co-conspirators reveals the deception
jokes.explanation: Absurd revelation of boy-dog conspiracy. The argument between 'partners in crime' is well-structured and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What if I read every other word? 'One more time. Your hopeless. Try again. Numb skull!'
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to decode cryptic message
jokes.punchline: Hidden message insults reader's intelligence while containing grammar error
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about puzzle-solving tropes. The irony of grammar error in insult about intelligence adds extra layer.

jokes.text: That's not very nice. I will try again, but not because the wall told me to.
jokes.setup: Wall message insulted Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's stubborn independence even when following wall's advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - maintaining dignity while doing exactly what she was told. Her stubborn intelligence creates humor.

jokes.text: No. I'm not a smart nerd. I'm just a weak nerd.
jokes.setup: Martin asked if he solved the puzzle
jokes.punchline: Self-aware categorization of his nerd subtype
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character humor. Martin's precise self-categorization as 'weak nerd' vs 'smart nerd' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Because we do our sleuthing in Mother's Nissan Quest minivan.
jokes.setup: Secret society called 'Brethren of the Quest'
jokes.punchline: Mundane explanation deflating the mysterious name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of mysterious organization with mundane reality. Perfect brand-specific humor and character-appropriate practicality.

jokes.text: staged a phoney War of Independence to cover their search for the gem.
jokes.setup: Historical conspiracy about founding fathers
jokes.punchline: Reducing American Revolution to elaborate treasure hunt cover story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist historical revision. Taking conspiracy theories to logical extreme by making entire revolution a cover story.

jokes.text: Four of which I own.
jokes.setup: Bell was melted down to make Civil War chess sets
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's irrelevant personal collection boast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - turning serious exposition into opportunity to brag about obscure collection. Very in character.

jokes.text: Well, well. If it isn't the Tardy Boys and Nancy Clueless.
jokes.setup: Burns encountering the mystery solvers
jokes.punchline: Burns' old-fashioned insults referencing dated pop culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns-style dated references. 'Nancy Clueless' is particularly good character-specific wordplay mixing Nancy Drew with modern slang.

jokes.text: That's my eyeball on the dollar bill. That's also my pyramid.
jokes.setup: Burns claiming to be old freemason
jokes.punchline: Personal ownership claims of illuminati symbols
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns megalomania. Claiming ownership of conspiracy symbols fits his character perfectly while being absurdly specific.

jokes.text: I keep telling you I'm not an albino! I just use a lot of sun block.
jokes.setup: Burns calling Smithers his 'faithful albino'
jokes.punchline: Smithers' defensive correction about his paleness
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Burns' casual insensitivity and Smithers' dignity. Running gag territory but still effective.

jokes.text: Then why do I give you all the albino holidays off?
jokes.setup: Smithers denying being albino
jokes.punchline: Burns' logical follow-up implying existence of albino-specific holidays
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic. The idea of albino-specific holidays is hilariously specific and shows Burns' completely warped worldview. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Superman, I have believed in you for years. If you can hear me now, please come help me dig this giant grave.
jokes.setup: Skinner forced to dig his own grave
jokes.punchline: Earnest prayer to fictional superhero for help with burial preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - earnest belief in pop culture mixed with tragic situation. The specificity of the request makes it brilliant.

jokes.text: So, the gem is inside you?
jokes.setup: Learning Lisa is 'the gem'
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of metaphorical concept
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal-mindedness. Missing metaphorical meaning for physical interpretation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Does kissing Milhouse count? No.
jokes.setup: Burns asking if Lisa is pure
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent question about childhood romance gets dismissed
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with Lisa's innocence. Burns' dismissive response shows his adult perspective versus child concerns.

jokes.text: How brilliant of God to remove my scepticism by making me his chosen one!
jokes.setup: Lisa discovering she's the prophesied gem
jokes.punchline: Lisa's instant abandonment of skepticism when it benefits her ego
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight. Shows Lisa's intellectual pride overriding her usual skepticism. Perfect hypocrisy revelation.

jokes.text: I don't really want you people inside, but you could dangle from the skids.
jokes.setup: Offering helicopter ride to heroes
jokes.punchline: Burns' reluctant help with classist accommodation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns class consciousness. Even when helping, maintains social hierarchy through seating arrangements.

jokes.text: Oh, fine, fine. Finish your song. I can be patient. After all, I am the gem child.
jokes.setup: Nuns singing dramatic choral piece
jokes.punchline: Lisa's impatient tolerance justified by her special status
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Lisa's ego inflation. Her condescending patience shows how quickly power corrupted her usual empathy.

jokes.text: Oh, please. How many sentences start with the word regally?
jokes.setup: Disputing Lisa's anagram solution
jokes.punchline: Logical criticism of forced word choice in puzzle
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on forced puzzle construction. Mother Superior's logical criticism of the anagram is genuinely funny.

jokes.text: This one?
jokes.setup: Mother Superior saying few sentences start with 'regally'
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out the current sentence does exactly that
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor. Lisa's logical trap catches Mother Superior in self-contradiction with perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: It's really Maggie, Sherlock.
jokes.setup: Wrong anagram solution revealed
jokes.punchline: Correct solution with condescending detective reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff with Mother Superior's smug victory. 'Sherlock' adds nice condescending touch to the reveal.

jokes.text: You know, I have to point out that this only makes sense if I get the wrong answer first.
jokes.setup: Realizing the puzzle structure
jokes.punchline: Lisa's meta-awareness of dramatic storytelling requirements
jokes.explanation: Exceptional meta-commentary on plot structure. Lisa breaking fourth wall to criticize story logic is brilliant and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Just set it to 'massage'.
jokes.setup: Cell phone ringing during peaceful moment
jokes.punchline: Casual solution to turn phone into massage device
jokes.explanation: Modern technology humor about phone vibration. Clever practical solution but not deeply character-driven.

jokes.text: Just set it to 'massage'.
jokes.setup: Phone keeps ringing during peaceful moment
jokes.punchline: Casual suggestion to change ringer to massage mode
jokes.explanation: Simple sight gag about phone settings during supposedly peaceful era. Good economy but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Again.
jokes.setup: Marge saying God wouldn't ask mother to sacrifice child
jokes.punchline: Single word acknowledging biblical precedent (Abraham/Isaac)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy. Single word reference to Abraham/Isaac story is devastating and shows deep biblical knowledge. Near-perfect craft.

jokes.text: Step aside, ladies. I'm bringing the peace.
jokes.setup: Another baby is placed on throne as gem child
jokes.punchline: Ralph's confident declaration about bringing peace
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - innocent confidence about bringing world peace. Great character voice and absurdist logic.

jokes.text: I'm so staying up pass my bedtime.
jokes.setup: Ralph as substitute gem child on throne
jokes.punchline: Ralph's excited mispronunciation and childish priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - mispronunciation plus missing the magnitude of his situation for small rebellion. Very in character.

tmdb_overview: Lisa infiltrates a convent in order to steal back Maggie who was accidentally taken in by nuns. While in the convent, Lisa uncovers a series of cryptic clues leading to a hidden jewel in Springfield. With help from resident history buffs Principal Skinner and Comic Book Guy, Lisa sets off to find the coveted prize. But Springfield's Freemasons are also hot on the trail of the jewel, and Lisa must race against them to find the jewel and rescue Maggie.
